§6Industrial Oil§f

§aA versatile intermediate from heavy oil — reclaim it, crack it, or fraction it into heating oil and lubricant!§f

Industrial Oil is a refined product obtained by processing §bHeavy Oil§f in the §bFractioning Tower§f. Every §e500mB§f of Heavy Oil yields §e350mB§f of Industrial Oil alongside §e150mB§f of Bitumen. It has low fuel value (§e82.5 kTU§f / §e103 kHE§f per bucket) and is best processed further.

§6Production§f:
§b• Fractioning Tower§f (primary): §e500mB§f Heavy Oil → §e350mB§f Industrial Oil + §e150mB§f Bitumen.
§b• Fractioning Tower§f (from Vacuum Heavy Oil): §e100mB§f → §e40mB§f Industrial Oil + §e60mB§f Heavy Heating Oil.

§6Uses§f:
§b• Reclaimed Industrial Oil§f: §e1,000mB§f Industrial Oil in §bChemical Plant§f → §e800mB§f Reclaimed Industrial Oil (for Petroil).
§b• Fractioning Tower§f: §e100mB§f Industrial Oil → §e60mB§f Heating Oil + §e40mB§f Engine Lubricant.
§b• Catalytic Cracking Tower§f: §e100mB§f Industrial Oil + Steam → §e60mB§f Naphtha + §e40mB§f Petroleum Gas.
§b• Coker Unit§f: §e7,900mB§f Industrial Oil → §e1×§f Petroleum Coke + §e790mB§f Coker Oil.
§b• Industrial Solidification§f: §e21,000mB§f → §e1×§f Solid Fuel.
§b• Pyrolysis Oven§f: §e8,700mB§f → §e1×§f Solid Fuel.

§eTip§f: Industrial Oil is renewable, making it a sustainable feedstock. Use the §bReclaimed Industrial Oil§f route to produce §bPetroil§f, which is a key ingredient in high-performance chemical processing.
